title: Infrared Cliff Detection for Robot Vacuums
content: Downward-facing infrared (IR) emitter–detector pairs detect floor discontinuities (stairs, drops) by measuring the drop in reflected IR intensity when the floor surface is absent or far away.
extended_content: Each cliff sensor consists of an IR LED and a phototransistor or photodiode angled at ≈ 15–45° from vertical.  Floor proximity: reflected signal is strong; cliff detected: reflected signal falls below threshold.  A typical robot vacuum has 4 cliff sensors (corners) operating at 38 kHz modulation to reject ambient light. Failure mode: dark or highly absorptive carpet reduces reflectance; threshold calibration per floor type is required.  Cliff detection triggers immediate motor stop and reverse within ≈ 20 ms (safety requirement).
source: iRobot Open Interface Specification; Neato Robotics Technical Docs
